title: Skill docs: document after vs blocked_by (preference vs dependency) across SKILL.md and references
description:
Update the distributable ticks skill (skills/ticks/ is the SOURCE OF TRUTH — never touch any installed copy under ~/.claude) for the soft-ordering feature shipped in epic 4bt.

Semantics to document (decided, do not re-litigate):
- blocked_by = feasibility (hard): tick is never ready until blockers close. Use for true technical dependencies — including sequencing chosen to avoid same-file merge conflicts between epics (that is a real constraint, not a preference).
- after = preference (soft): ordering only, never gates readiness. tk next sorts soft-deferred candidates last but never hides them — when the preferred epic is infeasible (hard-blocked or gated), selection naturally skips ahead to the first feasible epic; no flag needed.
- Missing or closed after-targets are ignored.
- Surfaces: tk show renders an After: line; tk roadmap layers waves on the union of both edge types and annotates each kind distinctly; queued status comes from hard edges only — an epic whose only open predecessors are soft stays ready/active.

Work:
1. skills/ticks/SKILL.md, "Roadmaps (multi-epic work)" section (~line 139): rewrite the chain example to use --after for pure sequencing preference and --blocked-by only where B genuinely needs A. State the parallel-fronts consequence: multiple epics can be ready at once; the close-out task fleshes out the next FEASIBLE epic in soft order (update the close-out task template wording accordingly). Keep the just-in-time detailing rule unchanged.
2. skills/ticks/references/tk-commands.md: document --after on tk create and tk update (clearable with --after ""), with one skip-ahead example (b depends on long-running a; c after b proceeds in parallel with a).
3. skills/ticks/references/tick-patterns.md: in the partitioning/sequencing guidance (Step 3 area, ~line 45), add the rule: same-file overlap between ticks/epics -> blocked_by (hard); pure order preference -> after (soft).
4. Grep skills/ticks/ for any other roadmap guidance assuming blocked_by-only chains (e.g. claude-runner.md two-tier stopping rule references) and update mentions where the meaning changes. Do NOT restructure documents; minimal coherent edits.

Files likely touched: skills/ticks/SKILL.md, skills/ticks/references/tk-commands.md, skills/ticks/references/tick-patterns.md, possibly skills/ticks/references/claude-runner.md.
Verification: docs-only change; re-read each edited section for internal consistency (no contradiction with the semantics block above); confirm no edits outside skills/ticks/.
acceptance_criteria: SKILL.md Roadmaps section teaches the two edge types and parallel fronts; tk-commands.md documents --after on create/update; tick-patterns.md states merge-conflict sequencing is hard; no stale blocked_by-only roadmap guidance remains
closed_reason: Completed: SKILL.md teaches both edge types + parallel fronts; tk-commands.md documents --after with skip-ahead example; tick-patterns.md edge-type rule in Step 3; claude-runner.md next-feasible-epic phrasing
